focus: Cross-system mapping + boundary stress for GitNexus patterns against Public Code Graph, Memory causal graph, and Skill Advisor owners.

graphEvents.label: Public Code Graph Memory Skill Advisor owner boundaries
Boundary stress: hidden default group member

label: Adopt GitNexus phase DAG as Code Graph orchestration, not as a shared mutable graph accumulator.
Reject a unified Public graph spanning Code Graph, Memory, and Skill Advisor because each owner has different truth units and schemas.
Reject mutating rename near-term; GitNexus preview/apply semantics diverge and low-confidence text hits can over-apply.
Adapt GitNexus freshness/status presentation independently per subsystem rather than sharing a single freshness contract.
Feed GitNexus tool/resource affordances into Skill Advisor via existing derived signals and graph-causal edges, not new raw tool/resource entity kinds.
Code Graph owns structural code facts and change-safety traversal.
Memory owns causal lineage between memory records, not code process or contract indexing.
Skill Advisor owns skill routing evidence over allowed skill metadata edge/entity schemas.
Default group member selection is convenient in GitNexus but should become explicit ambiguity in Public routing.
Current Public implementation paths use code_graph and skill_advisor underscores; earlier notes with hyphenated paths are stale.

direction: shared graph for code memory and skills
reason: Would collapse distinct Public owner schemas and make Memory a duplicate code index.
Preview/apply semantics are not exact-range equivalent in GitNexus evidence.
